texto: At the same time, eroticism in the home requires active engagement and willful intent. It is an ongoing resistance to the message that marriage is serious, more work than play; and that passion is for teenagers and the immature. We must unpack our ambivalence about pleasure, and challenge our pervasive discomfort with sexuality, particularly in the context of family. Complaining of sexual boredom is easy and conventional. Nurturing eroticism in the home is an act of open defience.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Esther Perel sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “At the same time, eroticism in the home requires active engagement and willful intent. It is an o…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, boredom, eroticism, útil em momentos de casamento e trabalho.

texto: In my work, I see couples who no longer wait for an invitation into their partner's interiority, but instead demand admittance, as if they are entitled to unrestricted access into the private thoughts of their loved ones

texto: But when we reduce sex to a function, we also invoke the idea of dysfunction. We are no longer talking about the art of sex; rather, we are talking about the mechanics of sex. Science has replaced religion as the authority; and science is a more formidable arbiter. Medicine knows how to scare even those who scoff at religion. Compared with a diagnosis, what's a mere sin? We used to moralize; today we normalize, and performance anxiety is the secular version of our old religious guilt.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Esther Perel que provoca temas ligados a fe. Ao questionar “But when we reduce sex to a function, we also invoke the idea of dysfunction. We are no longer ta…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre fe, religion, religion-and-science, com foco em religion-and-science e science.

texto: Modern relationships are cauldrons of contradictory longings: safety and excitement, grounding and transcendence, the comfort of love and the heat of passion We want it all, and we want it with one person. Reconciling the domestic and the erotic is a delicate balancing act that we achieve intermittently at best. It requires knowing your partner while remaining open to the unknown, cultivating intimacy that respects privacy. Separateness and togetherness alternate, or proceed in counterpoint. Desire resists confinement, and commitment mustn't swallow freedom whole.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Esther Perel sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “Modern relationships are cauldrons of contradictory longings: safety and excitement, grounding an…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sucesso, love, relationships, útil em momentos de termino e relationships.

texto: When we are children, play comes to us naturally, but our capacity for play collapses as we age. Sex often remains the last arena of play we can permit ourselves, a bridge to our childhood. Long after the mind has been filled with injunctions to be serious, the body remains a free zone, unencumbered by reason and judgment. In lovemaking, we can recapture the utterly uninhibited movement of the child, who has not yet developed self-consciousness before the judging gaze of others.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Esther Perel sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “When we are children, play comes to us naturally, but our capacity for play collapses as we age. …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, sex, sexuality, útil em momentos de sexuality.
